commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The next action is contributor proof or an explicit maintainer proof override, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff changes Codex provider result shaping and tests only; it does not broaden credential access, secret handling, dependency resolution, scripts, or supply-chain behavior. commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Codex web dashboard fallback that returns a credits-only usage snapshot after authorized attachment, adds focused regression coverage, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. for source reproduction: current main reaches `.missingUsage` when an authorized dashboard has no usage windows, and the linked report describes the credits-only authenticated state. I did not run a live provider probe because AGENTS.md says real account probes must be explicitly requested.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor proof or an explicit maintainer proof override, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix: preserve Codex web credits-only refresh
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve authorized Codex web dashboard snapshots when credits are present but usage-limit windows are absent
- synthesize a credits-only Codex `UsageSnapshot` with the attached dashboard identity, matching existing CLI/OAuth partial-result behavior
- add regression coverage for the authorized credits-only web dashboard path

## Why
Fixes #1204.

The Codex web strategy already verifies dashboard ownership before attachment, but it still threw `.missingUsage` when `dashboard.toUsageSnapshot(...)` returned nil. That made a useful authenticated dashboard response with credits/account data look like a failed provider fetch. CLI and OAuth already preserve credits-only responses, so this brings the web path in line with those sources.

## Validation
- `swift test --filter CodexWebDashboardStrategyAuthorityTests`
- `make check`
- `TZ=UTC swift test`
target.author: soumikbhatta

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
